affordances.name: Make or break interaction map
affordances.mechanism: When no single cause explains the outcome, require the answer to identify the few interacting forces whose sequencing, fit, or mutual reinforcement could create disproportionate upside or cascading downside.

use_when: The visible result comes from several reinforcing forces rather than one dominant driver.
Interventions will interact instead of landing independently.
The decision requires designing for emergent lift or emergent fragility.
do_not_use_when: Adding more variables mainly makes the analysis sound sophisticated.
A simpler model predicts equally well with fewer interacting terms.
case_evidence_needed: The sequencing or fit that makes the bundle different from independent actions.
The lollapalooza upside or cascading downside mechanism.
The make-or-break interaction that deserves attention first.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to isolate the few interactions that could create emergent lift or fragility, rather than expanding the variable list for sophistication.
evidence_required: The mechanism by which they reinforce or interfere.
The downside or upside if the interaction compounds.
good_output_shape: Explains why the forces do not act independently.
Distinguishes emergent effects from variable clutter.
States which interaction should be monitored or tested first.

diagnostic_questions: Which few forces interact in a way one-cause reasoning would miss?
Does sequencing or mutual reinforcement change the result materially?
Are we finding make-or-break interactions or just adding complexity?
misuse_guards: Do not make the analysis more complex mainly to sound sophisticated.
Do not ignore simpler explanations that predict equally well.
Do not treat every variable as equally important.

source_evidence.source_quote: Most useful when no single cause explains the outcome
Works when interventions will interact instead of landing independently
Best when the decision requires designing for emergent lift or emergent fragility

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 9 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports one combinatorial-effects affordance focused on make-or-break interactions, sequencing, and emergent upside/downside.
dropped_material: Did not extract general multidisciplinary latticework or symphony material as a separate card.
Kept lollapalooza and orchestration language as support for interaction mapping.
open_questions: PR43 should test whether this card catches interacting downside without becoming complexity theater.

absence_records.reason: The source warns that combination thinking misfires when teams add interacting variables mainly to sound sophisticated.

source_evidence.source_quote: Danger When Combination Thinking Becomes Complexity Worship

absence_records.reason: The source says over-investigation delays action and that the answer should focus on critical make-or-break hypotheses first.

source_evidence.source_quote: Focus on the critical, "make or break" hypotheses first.
multiple factors or forces, when aligned and interacting, produce an outcome that is disproportionately larger or more complex than the sum of their individual contributions

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 9 source reading found strong reviewed depth for combinatorial-effects as a make-or-break interaction map.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether this card helps the next LLM find emergent fragility without bloating packets.
